p-list.p-item: Refactored the runtime architecture by separating RetroArch rendering and game logic, with game logic now running on an independent thread for improved frame stability and responsiveness.
Added fast-forward support with 2x / 3x / 4x / 6x speed levels, reducing repetitive wait time and improving gameplay efficiency.
Integrated signalsmith-stretch for audio smoothing during fast-forward, effectively reducing noise artifacts and improving overall listening quality.
Upgraded configuration to a dual-layer model (core-level + game-level): set defaults per core and override them per game for finer-grained personalization.
Added a new SpriteKit-based Overlay buttons system to improve the in-game control and interaction experience.
Added smart multi-file ROM import detection for both file import and folder import workflows.
The importer can now automatically recognize grouped game files such as cue, m3u, gdi, mds, and ccd, detect missing referenced files, and handle grouped imports more reliably.
Enhanced data security by migrating core files (Roms, Save States, Databases, etc.) to a secure system directory, preventing accidental deletion and ensuring a more stable app experience.
RetroGo was officially released on the App Store.
